element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each vehicle has distinct center-of-gravity and handling characteristics; F-Type rear weight requires mastery of tail-drift cornering
Standard arcade racing controls; L/R buttons enable sharper turns
Strategic boost usage affects vehicle weight dynamics and collision damage
Polygon-based course design with vertical and horizontal variation; vehicle weight affects bump handling

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early race encounters with obstacles/other racers
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Vehicle weight and body characteristics determine collision impact severity
Additional vehicle option with distinct handling properties
Semi-trailer truck with unique weight/acceleration characteristics; high-skill content

typical_relief_triggers: Boost recharge periods between acceleration phases
Safe driving zones away from obstacles
typical_tension_triggers: Tight corners requiring L/R button precision
Road bumps and terrain variation affecting handling
Collision risk with competitors or environmental hazards
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade racing with emphasis on vehicle weight/momentum dynamics; tension builds through increasingly complex 3D track geometry and handling demands requiring vehicle-specific mastery

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to vehicle handling; basic racing with straightforward track layouts; emphasis on control mastery
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased 3D complexity with road bumps and overhead passes; vehicle-specific dynamics become critical; boost and collision management required
difficulty_curve.late_game: Bonus games and unlockable content; Trailer vehicle with extreme weight/acceleration characteristics; test-driving and time-attack modes
notable_spikes: F-Type tail-drift cornering mechanics (requires center-of-gravity mastery)
Transition from racing to obstacle courses (mode change, not difficulty spike)
Bonus games with semi-trailer truck handling

notable_level_patterns.description: Each vehicle's unique center of gravity, tire force application, and body mass creates emergent difficulty scaling; heavier vehicles (4WD, Trailer) are clumsier but have distinct advantages; lighter vehicles drift more easily

Courses constructed entirely of 3D polygons with road bumps, overhead passes, and detailed billboards; vertical variation in track design creates organic difficulty progression as player encounters spatial complexity

Game features five distinct modes (three racing variants, obstacle courses, test-drive, time-attack, multiplayer); each mode offers separate progression/mastery paths rather than sequential level gates

sources_used: wikipedia: Stunt Race FX (Gameplay, Development sections)
